mappings.rationaleAI: Cinema as medium, art form, and technology addresses the art of motion pictures (A), classification (A.1), component arts (A.2), and nonaesthetic contexts (C).
Cinema history covering technological transitions addresses the art of motion pictures (A), classification (A.1), technology in the creative process (A.2.e), and the motion-picture industry (C.1).
Hollywood film industry covering studio systems, star culture, and blockbuster models addresses the motion-picture industry (C.1) and motion-picture production (A.3).
Silent cinema's development from technological novelty to narrative storytelling addresses the art of motion pictures (A) and the use of technology in the creative process (A.2.e).
Film noir as a genre covering distinctive visual and narrative styles addresses classification of motion pictures (A.1) and the interrelation of other arts in motion pictures (B).

Documentary film as a genre concerned with representing reality addresses classification of motion pictures (A.1). Use as media of education and propaganda addresses functions of motion pictures (C.2).
Film music covering composition techniques and the relationship between music and narrative directly addresses the interrelation of other arts (music) in motion pictures (B).

Indian cinema covering Bollywood and regional traditions addresses classification (A.1), the motion-picture industry (C.1), and cinema's role in addressing social issues as education and propaganda (C.2).
French cinema covering the New Wave and cinematic writing addresses classification (A.1), the role of the director (A.2.c), interrelation with literature and painting (B), and the industry (C.1).
British cinema covering films, studios, and artistic language addresses classification (A.1) and the motion-picture industry (C.1).
Photography covering visual medium theory and documentary tradition addresses the use of technology in the creative process including the camera (A.2.e).
Physics of sound covering wave behavior and electronic production addresses the use of technology in the creative process including sound (A.2.e).
Dada and Surrealism covering photography and film as artistic media address the interrelation of other arts in motion pictures (B).
Horror spanning film and literature addresses classification of motion pictures (A.1) and the study and appreciation of motion pictures (C.3).
